danger_signals.visual_form: Skeletal creatures, demon dogs, goblin guards, harpies, dark knights positioned on playfield
danger_signals.meaning: Threats to overcome via ball physics; defeat required to progress

danger_signals.visual_form: Beezelbub sprite (Dark Lord of the Machine) in throne room stage
danger_signals.meaning: Final danger; represents kidnapper of princess and primary antagonist

danger_signals.visual_form: Tomb, Gate, Goblins, River, Harpy, Throne Room progression
danger_signals.meaning: Escalating danger; visual environment communicates increased difficulty
Failure state; player must maintain ball in play to survive encounters

safety_signals.visual_form: Dual flippers at bottom of playfield
safety_signals.meaning: Player control mechanism; upgraded flippers indicate safety improvement

safety_signals.visual_form: Upgraded stopper graphics marked '1 F P' (used version)
safety_signals.meaning: Ball retention upgrades purchasable at Black Market; defensive capability
Economic safety; enables purchases of defensive upgrades
Safe trading space; neutral ground for equipment acquisition

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Princess Bali (kidnapped goal), King (beaten in intro)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Throne Room; castle hierarchy; rescue objective

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Skeletal creatures, demon dogs, goblin guards, demon guards, harpies, dark knights
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Tomb, Gate, Goblins, River, Harpy stages; ascending castle interior
Black Market; trading post; embedded in gameplay progression

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: US intro sequence shows Queen in distress, King beaten; castle occupied by dark forces
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player must progress through six escalating stages; enemies block forward movement

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Stage progression (Tomb → Gate → Goblins → River → Harpy → Throne)
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Castle interior transitions through thematic locations; each stage features distinct enemy types and environmental hazards
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Difficulty escalation; new enemy types appear; castle ownership by dark lord becomes clearer

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Black Market access; flipper/stopper upgrade availability visual state
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Equipped upgrades alter ball physics responsiveness; stronger flippers enable new offensive strategies

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Beezelbub sprite dominates final stage; confrontation with Dark Lord
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Boss-specific ball targets; final mechanical challenge before rescue objective complete

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Japan version: Japanese characters in graphics; US version: Jaleco logo removed, copyright altered
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: US version includes narrative intro; both versions share core mechanical communication

environmental_storytelling.example: Tomb → Gate → Goblins → River → Harpy → Throne Room creates narrative arc through castle architecture; each stage name hints at enemy type and danger level
Captain's Spirit in Tomb, Ziffroo witch in Gate, goblin kids/guards in Goblins stage; enemies communicate location identity and occupant history
Escalating enemy complexity (skeletons → demons → goblins → dark knights → harpies → Beezelbub) communicates progression toward final threat
Wheel O' Luck and other stage-specific hazards embedded in pinball layout communicate environmental personality distinct from pure combat
Imps at Black Market indicate occupied castle economy; merchant activity suggests ongoing civilization despite dark lord occupation
Entry through Tomb and Goblins stages, progression to central River and Harpy sections, culminating in Throne Room—spatial hierarchy mirrors authority structure

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Ball physics-driven movement; responsive to flipper input; gravity-bound; objective seeking (hitting targets)
Positioned as static or semi-mobile obstacles on playfield; require ball contact/targets for defeat; block progression paths
Occupies throne room; requires specific ball interactions to defeat; represents climactic challenge distinct from standard enemies
Remain stationary at trading location; enable commerce without combat; neutral faction behavior despite dark lord occupation
Represented narratively as kidnapped goal; occupies throne room; liberation triggers game completion/ending

notes: Pinball Quest uses hybrid visual/mechanical communication: the pinball physics engine itself becomes the danger/safety signal system. Danger is communicated through enemy placement and stage theming rather than explicit HUD indicators. The castle architecture and stage progression provide primary environmental storytelling; narrative context (kingdom in distress, princess rescue mission) is explicit in intro sequence (US version). Faction identity relies heavily on character design rather than color coding. The Black Market represents unique safe space within hostile environment—merchant neutrality is implicitly communicated through economic interaction. Unused graphics ('1 2 3' vs '1 F P' stoppers, 'GOD!! FINE' golf text) suggest iterative design but do not affect manifestation layer in released version. World state is primarily communicated through stage progression and available upgrades rather than HUD or explicit indicators. This game predates modern UI conventions; all world-state communication flows through mechanical/environmental channels.
